title: draba glabella
intro: Draba glabella, also known as the Rocky Mountain draba, is a small, annual plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Draba glabella include reducing inflammation, calming coughs, and soothing skin irritations, making it a valuable herb for treating conditions such as bronchitis and eczema. Herbal preparations of Draba glabella can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected areas.
